title: Fix 17 unique runtime-proven dict-access-on-model crashes (Layer 1)

title: [MUST] C2: enforce phase-order invariant (OPS blocked until RED_TEAM + audit.green)
description: PROVEN gap. orchestrator.py:495 dispatches ops with no check that red_team ran or audit.green re-derived true. User wants to STOP AND REVIEW before push, so ops/propose-only must not be reachable until red_team completed + audit.green (re-derived from risks, runner.py:817-836/946-972) is True. Add a server-side assert in the ops dispatch path that halts loudly if prior phases incomplete or audit not green. Scope: admin/orchestrator/ only. No fallback, no silent swallow.

title: [MUST] C2: enforce phase-order invariant before OPS
description: PROVEN gap. orchestrator.py:495 dispatches ops with no check that red_team completed or audit.green re-derived true. User wants to STOP and REVIEW before push, so ops/propose-only must not be reachable until red_team done + green. Add server-side assert in ops dispatch path (reuse runner.py:817-836 red_team_passed re-derivation). Fail loudly if violated.

title: [MUST] M7: mark loopguard recovery output, do not silently accept

BLOCKED phase recorded completed. Fix: annotate recovered output + surface (fail-loudly / review), never silently accept as clean pass.

title: Fix planner read-budget starvation + review gate (combines A+B+C surgical)

A=BUDGET: enforce READ_BUDGET/RECALL_BUDGET in GuardToolset + planner FATAL nudge before 5th read (runner.py planner path + planner.yaml). B=REVIEW GATE: supervisor_review/re-exec (runner.py:1463/1279) handles BLOCKED. References: q9lt/4mn8/6loaa/ba37. SURGICAL only; ruff after; src2 untouched.

title: Clash 冲开 releases hidden stems but they are NOT re-folded into favorability with 用/忌 valence, and surfacing is natal-only (drops da-yun + current)
description:
PRINCIPLE (user, 2026-07-12): 冲 opens the storehouse (冲开/库开) — hidden 人元 (treasures) surface and MUST be re-added to the calculation across the FULL board = natal 4 pillars + 大运 + current (流年/流月/流日) + surfaced hidden stems. Good/bad of each surfaced element depends on 用神/忌神.

VERIFIED GAPS:
1) Surfacing is NATAL-ONLY: handle_clash_activation (orchestrator.py:130-149) builds chart_branches from the 4 natal pillars only; the 'if branch in chart_branches' guard drops hidden stems of any 大运/流年 clashed branch. calculate_clash_adjusted_dm_score (module2_root.py:593-654) loops ONLY ['year/month/day/hour_pillar'] — da_yun_pillar and current pillars excluded; hidden_ratio=1.0 only for clashed NATAL branches.
2) Surfaced treasures NOT folded into favorability with valence: clash_activation_results -> display only (orchestrator.py:741); dm_strength_clash -> display only (line 745) and is DM-strength anyway (rk6l: config selector, must not be favorability value); check_tomb_clash_trigger 'releases hidden elements' -> ten_gods_results['tomb_clash_trigger'] display only (line 515).
3) No 用/忌 valence labeling: surfaced hidden stems counted only elementally into root/support/control to DM (module2_root.py:642-647) = DM-strength (rk6l anti-pattern), never as medicine/taboo.

DETECTION scope IS correct: clashes span natal vs 大运 (lu_clash) vs current (day_clash/冲太岁) in module1_macro/module9/module11. Only the SURFACING + re-scoring is broken.

ACCEPTANCE: when any branch is clashed (natal<->natal, natal<->dayun, natal<->current), surface its hidden stems at 100% and ADD them to the active-element set; active board = natal 4 + dayun + current + surfaced hidden; each surfaced hidden stem labeled 用/忌 and scored with valence (mirror module12 + bead rgmv); 库冲 releasing 用神 -> favorable, releasing 忌神 -> mixed (relief vs instability).

CROSS-REFS: rgmv (clash valence sign), rk6l (strength-as-value), ykya (void same family), module12_compatibility.py:315-363 (valence pattern).

title: yrev-A: convert unified.py core domain dict fields to typed models
description:
Part of baziforecaster-yrev (100% Pydantic engine). Convert ALL (i) untyped dict[str,Any] and (ii) typed scalar domain-map fields in src2/core/schemas/unified.py to strict Pydantic models. FIELDS (file:line:class.field:type):
- 271 SessionState.profile_data: dict[str,Any] -> type it; ALSO DROP model_config={'extra':'allow'} from SessionState (line ~274).
- 360 DailyForecastRecord.activities: dict (untyped) -> typed.
- 362 DailyForecastRecord.hourly_scores: dict[str,dict[str,float]]|None -> typed.
- 417 ChartProfile.elemental_concentration: dict[str,float]|None -> typed (ElementalMap: wood/fire/earth/metal/water:float).
- 443 TracePayload.data: dict[str,Any] -> trace envelope; inspect orchestrator.py:186-187 for stored keys, convert to a typed TracePayloadData model OR if truly dynamic/debug, flag in PR notes (do NOT leave dict[str,Any]).
- 474 InteractionOutput.clash_details: dict[str,str] -> typed.
- 495 GeJuInput.transformed_branches: dict[str,str]|None -> typed.
- 622 TenGodsInput.stems: dict[str,str] -> typed.
- 623 TenGodsInput.branches: dict[str,str] -> typed.
- 627 TenGodsOutput.ten_gods_map: dict[str,str] -> typed.
- 654 ScoringInput.penalties: dict[str,float] -> typed.
- 738 SpectrumInput.transformed_branches: dict[str,str]|None -> typed.
- 743 SpectrumOutput.elemental_scores: dict[str,float] -> typed (ElementalMap).
- 748 SpectrumOutput.san_de: dict[str,str]|None -> typed.
- 749 SpectrumOutput.sub_scores: dict[str,float|None]|None -> typed.
- 840 ContradictionClassification.sources: dict[str,str] -> typed.
- 841 ContradictionClassification.elements: dict[str,str|None] -> typed.
- 919 ContradictionEntry.classical_resolution: dict[str,str]|None -> typed (inspect contradiction_resolver.py usage for keys; model with winner/explanation/...).
- 960 ActivityDayResult.activities: dict[str,ActivityScore|float] -> typed.
- 964 ActivityDayResult.hourly_scores: dict[str,dict[str,float]]|None -> typed.
- 2744 Module4Results.potency_by_domain: dict[str,int] -> typed (DomainPotencyMap).
- 2774 RiskFactor.event_signals: dict[str,float] -> typed.
- 2779 RiskFactor.event_probabilities: dict[str,float] -> typed.
- 2796 DmStrengthTier1.components: dict[str,float] -> typed.
- 2813 DayHourTenGodEmphasis.emphasis_scores: dict[str,float] -> typed.
RULES: Define small reusable map models in unified.py (e.g. ElementalMap for per-element float maps; BranchMap/StemMap for branch/stem maps; DomainPotencyMap). Reuse across fields. Place new models NEAR related classes. Update EVERY access site in src2/ (grep each field name + parent attr; fix .get/[key]/items/keys/values and construction kwarg dicts). Do NOT edit src2/engine/* (that is yrev-B/C). Acceptance: no dict[str,Any] or domain dict[str,X] remains in these unified.py fields; ruff clean on src2/core/schemas/unified.py; all imports resolve; 'uv run python -c import' of unified works. NEVER git commit/push.

No code edits.

title: [GOOD] M9: CJK-aware token estimate for compaction gate
description: PROVEN. _loopguard.py:376-382 estimate_tokens uses char_div_4 (config control_orchestrator.py:575); no CJK-aware tokenizer. Chinese-heavy payloads under-estimated 2-4x -> compaction fires too late -> ContextWindowExceeded. Use tiktoken cl100k or CJK floor. Prevents mid-run crash.

title: [GOOD] M12: freeze per-message record-time timestamp in history persist
description: PROVEN. converter.py:78 ts computed once per messages_to_md; artefacts.py:321 whole-file rewrite -> all msgs same rewrite-time stamp. Freeze timestamp at record time in persist step so planner.md timeline is accurate.

title: [GOOD] M3: enrich status board + per-task update during EXECUTE

description: PROVEN. maybe_compact _loopguard.py:385-454 never writes phase_summaries (docstring says NEVER); L3 fed only from terse raw role output (runner.py:1152/1191). Wire summarizer output into phase_summaries for richer cross-phase context. Lower priority than M9.

title: Read-Bucket Protocol (declare-then-fetch) build
description: Infra-level redesign of agent tool access to kill the 15-call research loop. Replace inline read_file/search/investigate with: (1) a spawn-time repo map (tree+symbols+KG, src2/file_paths scoped), (2) a declare-gated batch_read tool (max 20 files/call, mandatory line_ranges, 5 attempts uniform), (3) write tools returning diff. read_file kept coder-only (10, pre-edit). Exhaustion -> planner-style FATAL nudge + strip read tools -> forced final_result. Philosophy: fix the tool surface, not the model. Full spec: admin/orchestrator/docs/03a_build.md

title: RBP-1: batch_read tool + GuardToolset read_budget counter
description: Add batch_read(paths, line_ranges) to TOOL_REGISTRY (internally calls existing read_file/investigate CLI wrappers, bundles into ONE message, wrap_with_acl on file_paths). Add read_budget counter (uniform 5) + [READ a/5] marker to GuardToolset. Cap 20 files/call, line_ranges mandatory. Output -> file_cache + scoped anchor (no full-file inline). Touch: tools.py TOOL_REGISTRY, GuardToolset, wrap_with_acl; artefacts.py file_cache.

title: RBP-2: spawn-time repo map injection (tree+symbols+KG)
description: In build_worker_spec/load_skill, before agent.run, build and prepend a rich map = get_repo_structure + get_file_symbols + query_knowledge_graph, scoped to task file_paths (src2, NOT repo-wide). This map REPLACES search/investigate. Touch: tools.py load_skill/build_worker_spec; control_orchestrator.py (scope config).

title: RBP-3: remove search/investigate; read_file coder-only
description: Remove search/investigate from ALL role allow-lists (map substitutes). Remove raw read_file from non-coder roles; keep read_file for coder only (10 tries, pre-edit read of exact file in hand). Touch: tools.py TOOL_REGISTRY, build_worker_spec, SKILL_MAP/customised yamls.

title: RBP-5: read_budget exhaustion -> FATAL nudge + strip read tools
description: At read_budget=0 with no final_result: inject 'You have finished reading. Produce your output now.' AND strip read tools (tools=[] for deepseek_flash which lacks tool_choice) to force final_result. Reuse RECOVER-not-EXIT (_loopguard.py) + planner _FATAL_BUDGET (tools.py:339) pattern. No new retry loop, no hang. Touch: tools.py GuardToolset, _loopguard.py.

title: Fix orchestrator crash: role->output-type registry regression from pqr2 refactor
description: run_orchestrator.sh crashes immediately: load_skill checks 'if role not in OUTPUT_TYPE_REGISTRY' but that registry is keyed by output-type NAME (DraftPlan...) not role name (supervisor_plan...). So every role returns '[HALT] unknown role'. build_role_agent also does OUTPUT_TYPE_REGISTRY[role] at runner.py:407/646. Root cause: pqr2 collapsed the role->model map into a type-name->model registry without adding a role->output-type-name bridge. Fix: add ROLE_OUTPUT_TYPE (role->type name) derived from SKILL_MAP in common/registry.py; update runner.py:407/590/646 to use it.

title: Root-cause: only 3 coder files spawned for 7 planner subtasks
description: Planner issued 7 subtasks (coder_1..coder_7). supervisor_plan prompt (templates/supervisor_plan.yaml:22) forces 'reject at least one rejected_subtask when plan > 6 subtasks'. Model satisfied this by MERGING coder_3..coder_7 into a single task with 5 file_paths instead of rejecting them -> only 3 ApprovedPlan tasks -> 3 coder_1/2/3 files. ROOT: the >6 rejection rule implicitly encourages merging. FIX: when plan>6 subtasks, the rule should forbid silent merge-to-fewer-tasks; require rejected_subtasks to be EXPLICIT rejections (id+reason), not consolidation. Also coder naming a101k maps each ApprovedTask.id -> coderN folder, so merge directly reduces spawned coders.

title: Memory pollution: full ApprovedPlan dumped into every coder's message_history
description: runner.py:1024 stores PHASE_SUMMARIES[role]=_model_to_md(result.output) = full flat MD projection of the ENTIRE ApprovedPlan (all tasks, file_paths, instructions). This is prepended into EVERY coder's message_history (runner.py:848-856 prior_phase_summaries block). Each coder thus sees the whole plan + all sibling tasks = polluted, non-task-specific memory, and bloats context. FIX: inject only the SINGLE task's own brief context into each coder (task-scoped), not the global PHASE_SUMMARIES. The coder brief already carries its own task; prior_phase_summaries should be suppressed or scoped for the coder role.

title: [FIX] supervisor_plan merge-collapse + coder memory pollution (2 surgical fixes)
description:
Two surgical fixes to stop (1) supervisor_plan collapsing planner subtasks into fewer tasks, and (2) plan-wide context polluting every coder's memory.

ROOT CAUSE 1 (merge): planner issued 7 subtasks (coder_1..coder_7). templates/supervisor_plan.yaml:22 forces 'reject at least one rejected_subtask when plan > 6 subtasks'. The model satisfied this by MERGING coder_3..coder_7 into a single task with 5 file_paths -> only 3 ApprovedPlan tasks -> 3 coder_1/2/3 files. Confirmed in artefacts/workplan/planner_sup/planner_sup.json (3 tasks).

ROOT CAUSE 2 (memory pollution): runner.py:848-856 injects PHASE_SUMMARIES['supervisor_plan'] (= full flat MD dump of entire ApprovedPlan, all tasks/file_paths/instructions, set at runner.py:1024 _model_to_md) as prior_phase_summaries block into EVERY coder's load_skill brief. Coders go via record_coder -> load_skill('coder', task_scoped_brief) and do NOT receive the global brief accumulator (prior_role_outputs, runner.py:2253), so this block is the SOLE plan-wide pollution source. Each coder sees the whole plan + all sibling tasks = bloated, non-task-specific memory.

PLANNER-RECEIVES-SUP-COMMENTS: VERIFIED WORKING. run_gated('planner','supervisor_plan') (runner.py:2468) re-runs do_role('planner') on sup FAIL; do_role folds reviewer output into brief via prior_role_outputs block (runner.py:2253-2263) which renders phase_summaries['supervisor_plan'] (incl rejected_subtasks). No change needed there.

INVARIANT (confirmed): 1 coder_N = 1 ApprovedTask; 1 task = many file_paths (models.py:79). Planner owns file-disjointness (parallelisable_workplan); runner enforces + HALTs on cross-group collision (runner.py:1268, ticket zu9u). No silent reassign.

FIX 1: templates/supervisor_plan.yaml (~line 22). Rewrite the '>6 subtasks' rule so rejected_subtasks MUST be explicit named rejections with id+reason; merging subtasks into fewer tasks is PROHIBITED. Planner's N subtasks stay N (or are genuinely rejected, not consolidated).

FIX 2: runner.py:848-856. Skip the prior_phase_summaries block when role=='coder' (coders already get task-scoped brief built in run_execute_phase + own coderN.md memory bridge).

ACCEPTANCE: planner issues 7 subtasks -> supervisor_plan emits 7 (or explicit rejections, never 3 merged); coder_1..coder_7 folders created; coder .md brief contains only its own task, no sibling tasks' file_paths/instructions. ruff clean. No src2 edits.

title: gold suite: HarnessProbe Intercept misses UnexpectedModelBehavior (malformed tool-args)
description: FINDING (gold-suite build, 2026-07-14). BIFR Step 2 (Intercept) is only partially covered. pydantic-ai 2.3.0 wraps malformed tool-call ARGS on an output_type agent into UnexpectedModelBehavior('Exceeded maximum output retries') via consume_output_retry, which is NOT ValidationError/ModelRetry. So tests/_probe.py's (ValidationError, ModelRetry) handler never sees the most common real-world failure (LLM returns bad JSON for a tool). test_intercept currently works around this by raising ValidationError directly from the FunctionModel fn. FIX: extend HarnessProbe to also catch pydantic_ai.exceptions.UnexpectedModelBehavior and record it in validation_failures with the raw string, so Intercept truly captures malformed-tool-arg failures. ACCEPTANCE: a FunctionModel returning invalid ToolCallPart args is intercepted + fail_*.json written + probe.validation_failures records type 'UnexpectedModelBehavior'.

title: docs: testing_framework.md audit Status column is stale vs refactored code
description: FINDING (2026-07-14). admin/orchestrator/infra/testing_framework.md audit report is STALE: its Status column + line numbers describe a pre-refactor architecture that no longer exists (runner.py is now a gate-runner; no run_phase/run_orchestrator/execute_task/_write_transcript/FALLBACK_SHEET; _write_transcript removed; transcripts now tools.log_*/_loopguard._log_turn). This misled re-scanning and wasted effort. FIX: refresh the Status column against the current tree, OR mark the report HISTORICAL and point readers to the GOLD TEST SUITE section. ACCEPTANCE: Status column matches current code, or a clear HISTORICAL banner added + link to the adopted BIFR gold suite.

title: Planner prompt hardening + artefacts store for agent outputs/history
description: Scope: improve planner.yaml (parallelism up to 20 agents, AST-edit first, effort weights, no-shell ruff-as-acceptance) for the dict->pydantic migration; tighten the user prompt with exact src2/engine file paths and re-run scratch; wire improved planner into real runner (run_orchestrator); create artefacts/ folder tree (history/ + workplan/ per agent: planner, planner_sup, coder, coder_sup, red_team) to persist message history + agent outputs. No model changes; harness/infra only.

title: Redundant dead prompt dir: infrastructure/prompt/ (legacy factory.py) — remove after coder restore
description:
There are THREE prompt dirs under admin/orchestrator but only TWO are live.

LIVE (the intended 2-set):
- templates/<role>.yaml = frozen base skeletons (_BASE_/_GENERATED_ markers). forge_skill_spec() reads these (tools.py:937) to build customised/. This is the 'never changes' source.
- customised/<role>.yaml = forged runtime output. load_skill_spec() loads these (tools.py:1001). This is the 'forged at runtime' set.

DEAD / LEGACY (the extra 3rd dir):
- infrastructure/prompt/<role>.yaml = rich hand-authored role prompts loaded by factory.py. factory.py is DEAD CODE: NOT imported by runner.py or tools.py (only matches are in .venv lib code). Leftover from B2-era 'factory loads infrastructure/prompt/<role>.yaml -> RoleSpec' architecture, superseded by templates/+forge_skill_spec(). Contains coder/planner/red_team/supervisor_plan/supervisor_review/ops/summarizer yamls with rich instructions + model_key + execution_settings.
- prompt/forge.yaml = SkillForge meta-prompt DESIGN doc (M7/D8). Orphaned: the real forge_skill_spec() is 'No LLM - pure structural extraction' and does NOT consume forge.yaml. prompt/user_prompt.md is the live user task spec (separate concern).

KEY FINDING: the RICH coder prompt we need to restore (baziforecaster-8ycr) STILL EXISTS verbatim in infrastructure/prompt/coder.yaml (full 'ApprovedPlan... follow EXACTLY' + 'must_be_pydantic STRICT Pydantic... exact cells red team will fail you on' text). The live base templates/coder.yaml only has the thin _BASE_, which is why customised/coder.yaml got gutted (forge re-derived from thin skeleton; rich source never lived in templates/).

ALSO: prompt/forge.yaml is currently an orphan design doc (not consumed by forge_skill_spec). Decide whether to wire it or delete.

title: REPO SCAN: persist ALL agents' history at every stage (infra)
description: Broad repo scan of admin/orchestrator infra to guarantee artefacts/history/<role>/*.jsonl is written for EVERY role (planner, planner_sup, coder, coder_sup, red_team) at EVERY stage (PLAN, PLAN_REVIEW, EXECUTE, CODE_REVIEW, RED_TEAM, OPS) on BOTH success and failure. Must not regress: coder_sup (supervisor_review) history must be captured. Then produce a comprehensive fix plan. Refs: SA5-F2, forensic-coder-zero-output-ebgd. PLAN MODE — scan only, no edits.

title: Add EXECUTE phase logging and fix silent swallows (SA5-F2/F4)
description: SA5 gaps: No per-task transcript generated for the Coder/EXECUTE phase (F2) where hangs occur. Also, fail-dump mechanism silently swallows its own write errors (F4).

title: Harness propose-only: stage coder edits in temp/, exit after red-team
description: Per Francis (2026-07-17): coder must NOT write src2/ (ban fc7d91cf stands). Instead stage proposed edits under admin/orchestrator/temp/ mirroring repo paths. coder_sup + red_team review the temp/ copy vs src2/ original (read-only). Pipeline terminates after RED_TEAM; no OPS, no git push, no apply-to-src2. Human reviews temp/ and applies manually. Acceptance: a run produces proposed file(s) in temp/ and exits; src2/ untouched; red_team AuditResult present; no OPS/push phase runs.

title: Rewire run_orchestrator.sh + runner for --from coder continuation
description: Add a --from coder flag to run_orchestrator.sh and runner.py. When set: skip planner/supervisor_plan, run the flat coder<->supervisor_review and coder<->red_team gates, and re-seed the first coder pass from the persisted exchange file (admin/orchestrator/temp/<bd>_exchange.json). Preserve runtime state (stop the destructive wipe) so a re-launch continues. Propose-only stop after red_team.

title: Per-role history timestamp stamped at persist time, not per-turn record time
description: In admin/orchestrator/artefacts/history/planner/planner.md all 30 messages carry the IDENTICAL timestamp 2026-07-17-16:29:25. Spec context-mgmt-redesign-locked-decisions req4 requires per-turn RECORD-time timestamp 'YYYY-MM-DD-HH:MM:SS' local + monotonic msg N counter. The monotonic 'msg N' counter works but the timestamp is being applied at whole-file persist/rewrite time (message_history is cumulative per pydantic-ai 2.0 so persist = transform + whole-file rewrite of <role>.jsonl -> every message gets stamped 'now'). Fix: capture + freeze the timestamp at RECORD time per message (in the evict/record step in _loopguard.maybe_compact / converter), not at rewrite. Affects converter.py MD twin + JSONL source.

title: Implement budget-triggered bd compaction ritual (05_Upgrade)
description:
Port the OpenCode 'compact your memory' ritual into the harness: at the agent.run() seam, when context >= 200K tokens, tell the agent to externalize via bd remember, drop message_history, and reseed with [system]+[task brief]+[recalled remember facts] only. Collapses prior tickets #1 (Reflector/consolidation) and #3 (hard token budget) into ONE mechanism.

CONTRACT (admin/orchestrator/docs/05_Upgrade.md §2):
- CONTEXT_COMPACT_CEILING = 200_000  (trigger)
- CONTEXT_COMPACT_FLOOR    =  60_000  (hard assert after reseed)
- EMPTY_EXT_RETRIES        =   3       (gate)

RITUAL:
1. Estimate tokens of [system + task brief + message_history] after agent.run().
2. if >= CEILING: inject 'compact now — use bd remember, externalize what you need'.
3. model calls bd remember (IT decides). if empty: retry up to 3x; still empty -> HALT.
4. DROP message_history; reseed = [system] + [task brief] + [recalled remember facts].
5. HARD ASSERT reseed tokens < FLOOR (60K); if >= 60K -> HALT (thrash/corruption guard).
6. continue.

WHAT IT REPLACES: maybe_compact SINK-1 live summarizer (retire); separate Reflector ticket (#1); separate hard-token-budget ticket (#3).

MAPPING (03_Context_Management.md): trigger seam = maybe_compact hook in _loopguard.py; bd remember = admin/tools/remember_fact.py (TOOL_REGISTRY) — grant worker tool_allow_list on compaction turn only; reseed replaces Channel-3 _load_role_messages reload; recall_fact/bd show already exists.

FAIL-LOUDLY: empty externalization -> retry 3x then RuntimeError('[HALT] context compaction produced no durable memory'); floor violation -> RuntimeError('[HALT] post-compaction context >= 60K floor'). No silent swallow.

title: Per-task episodic bd retrieval (point #2, deferred)
description:
POINT #2 from the RAG deep-dive: replace broadcast-by-copy of the single global alignment fact with per-task EMBEDDING similarity retrieval over the remember_fact KB. At spawn, the HARNESS (not the model) embeds the task brief, cosine-top-k over fact embeddings, and PREPENDS the k most-similar facts into instructions=. Workers NEVER receive a retrieve tool (PUSH, not PULL) — single-writer invariant preserved, matches Auton §5.2.2 (runtime retrieves, agent receives).

WHY DEFERRED: only meaningful once the remember_fact KB has SCALE (many per-task lessons). Today the KB ~= 1 entry (global alignment) so top-k == today's broadcast. j8ow (#1+#3) is what POPULATES the KB via compaction externalization. So this is PHASE-2, blocked-by j8ow.

THREE INJECTION CHANNELS (post-j8ow):
1. History (already done) — this run's own <role>.jsonl reload.
2. Global fact (already done) — one key-recalled fact, broadcast to all.
3. #2 (new) — top-k similar past lessons, matched to THIS task by embedding, cross-run.

REUSE: BGEM3 embedding endpoint (http://127.0.0.1:8002/v1/embeddings, already used by the RAG query.py) for arbitrary text — no new model. Store embeddings in a sidecar JSONL {key,value,vector}; in-process cosine (KB tiny). New fn recall_fact_similar(brief,k=5). Replace runner.py:881 GLOBAL ALIGNMENT broadcast.

FAIL-LOUDLY: if BGEM3 down -> fall back to single global alignment fact (current behavior), never broadcast whole store. k too high -> bloat -> re-triggers j8ow; default k=5.

title: RBP-4: write tools return diff/changed region
description: write_file / replace_text / replace_function return the diff/changed region only (NOT full file). Eliminates post-write read_file re-read. Diff small enough for message_history; full content in file_cache. Touch: admin/tools/write_file.py, replace_text.py, replace_function.py.

title: RBP-4: write tools return diff changed region
description: write_file / replace_text / replace_function return the diff or changed region only, NOT the full file. Eliminates post-write read_file re-read. Diff small enough for message_history; full content in file_cache. Touch: admin/tools/write_file.py, replace_text.py, replace_function.py.

title: 05_Upgrade #2: per-task episodic bd retrieval (top-k by embedding similarity)
description: Source: 05_Upgrade.md §8 + 06_Implement.md Phase E. Retrieval half that makes the j8ow compaction reseed actually small. Harness-driven PUSH (not model pull): at spawn (build_worker_spec/load_skill) embed task_brief, cosine-top-k over remember_fact KB, prepend into instructions=. Worker stays bd-blind for READS. BLOCKED-BY j8ow (wkxy) - only meaningful once the KB has scale (~100 lessons from compactions). Today's single global-alignment key == current broadcast, so no observable change yet. Acceptance: recall_fact_similar(task_brief,k=5) wired at spawn; injected as RELEVANT MEMORY; ruff clean.

(2) Coverage gaps: main() conductor+plan-gate, run_code_review_gate, _blocker_findings_from_risks, DAG HALT/re-spawn, tools SRC-BAN + wrap_untrusted_task, control_orchestrator redaction+config, converter.py, http_client.py, compact_memory_gate, loopguard RECOVER branches, 4 modules (ledger, bazi_policy, bazirag_client, subagent_discipline). GOAL: upsize kit to cover all harness features deterministically with TestModel/FakeProc mocks.

title: Workstream C4: EngineOutput wrapper + downstream consumer typing (full zero-dicts at API boundary, B1)
description:
Workstream C4 (FINALIZED via grill-me + alignment session, 2026-07-12): ENGINE 100% PYDANTIC — type migration only.

SCOPE (in):
- EngineOutput.engine_outputs: dict[str,Any] -> EngineOutputs model (fields use each module's SPECIFIC output model; dict[str,Model] keyed collections allowed, e.g. activity_forecasts/selective_extractions/clash_activation — egress transformer handles them, NO wrapper container models per aligned Q2).
- EngineOutput.month_metadata: dict[str,Any] -> SolarMonthAnchor (orchestrator builds SolarMonthAnchor).
- Orchestrator builds EngineOutputs(...) directly (drops per-module .model_dump() where the field is a model); resolve_contradictions receives typed EngineOutputs and reads attributes (no .get).
- Inner-dict sweep: convert remaining dict[str,Any] in engine output/result models to typed models. DONE: ModuleRootOutput/InteractionOutput/MedicineOutput/PalaceOutput/ShenShaOutput/TriggersOutput/EventClassificationOutput/ProbabilityOutput. RESIDUAL (finish now, Q3): ChartProfile.macro_results/compatibility_profile, TraceEntry.inputs/outputs, ContradictionResult.contradictions/synthesis, ActivityDayResult.*.
- Move ScoreDesc/VoidEmptinessBreakdown/CompatibilityBreakdown/CompatibilityResult INTO unified.py (core); module12 imports from core (aligned Q1).

OUT OF SCOPE (explicitly deferred):
- Creating transformer.py / wrapper container models (rejected, Q2).
- Fixing the pre-existing _serialise_engine_outputs 'decade_data' AttributeError bug -> C5 (transformer owns it, Q4).
- C5 ingress massage layer (separate bead baziforecaster-wpsy).

title: Harness: residual pre-run defects from planner/supervisor eval
description:
Found during eval of planner.md / planner_sup.md (commit 77e1883e fixed the loopguard wire-tap leak; these remain):

1. PHANTOM TASK: epic deliverable #3 tells coder to convert stakeholders/metadata/conversation_history in src2/engine/session.py. Actual session.py is 87 lines and contains NONE of those fields (it is a tier-label mapper). Coder following orders literally either skips (safe) or invents a class (violation). Fix: drop/qualify deliverable #3 in the user_prompt spec, or confirm the real location.

2. CONTRADICTORY ACCEPTANCE #3: epic says 'no .get( on model-typed values' but DictMap (unified.py:256) DEFINES .get() itself and engine code calls it. The verify_dict_access_runtime.py scanner is the real arbitrator; acceptance #3 as worded would flag legitimate DictMap.get() as a violation. Fix: narrow acceptance #3 to raw-dict .get()/isinstance, exempt DictMap protocol methods.

3. batch_read FORMAT TAX WASTES CALLS: tool rejects comma-joined multi-range ('350-400,600-650') and empty line_ranges, forcing 4+ calls to cover unified.py regions. Planner+supervisor each burned 2 of 15 calls on these rejections before READ BUDGET EXHAUSTED. Fix: allow multi-segment ranges in batch_read, OR pre-inject the exact shim line-numbers into codebase_reference_context so planner does not spend calls hunting.

Note: model map sharing (all roles on same wavelength) is NOT a defect — only the un-patch-on-exit was. That is already fixed.

title: add run_orchestrator_continue.sh wrapper (start at coder)
description: Thin wrapper: ./admin/orchestrator/run_orchestrator_continue.sh execs run_orchestrator.sh --from coder, so operator can resume directly at the coder phase without remembering the flag. Suppresses destructive wipe and re-seeds coder from the persisted exchange file.

title: [KIV] M6: cost multiplier review (retries=5 knob)
description: INCONCLUSIVE. retries=5 real (tools.py:1275); outer loop capped MAX_LOOPGUARD_TURNS=20 (_loopguard.py:39,207); request_limit=40 not 20; _run_with_backoff/backoff=4 no longer exists. Revisit only if runaway-cost incident occurs.

title: [KIV] M1/M2: launcher wipe design is correct
description: RETRACTED. Two-launcher design correct: run_orchestrator.sh (fresh, wipes) + run_orchestrator_continue.sh (resume, never wipes, passes --from). No change needed. KIV/closed.

title: [KIV] C1/H4/H5/H7/M10: stale audit findings, not in code
description: All REFUTED by subagent code-read. C1 no run_orchestrator fn; H4 coder writes task_<id>.log; H5 eval.jsonl has usage+message_count; H7 models have min_length=1; M10 coder prompt rich in templates/customised. No action. KIV/closed-by-verification.
